calendar.matters:
Petition To Confirm Trust And Determine Ownership Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kulwinder Bains appeared by telephone for Nakul Narayan.

The Court noted that it received the supplemental information, that it had requested consents from the two Sons, that it can construe Nakul's declaration as a consent, but questions Tejas' declaration. Ms. Bains stated that both of the Sons are in agreement, that she thought the declarations supported that, that Tejas has been notified, and that Tejas has no problem with the petition and he has not objected. The Court noted that Nakul's declaration requests that the Court confirm the trust and appoint him as the sole Trustee, but Tejas' does not say anything like that. Ms. Bains confirmed that Tejas is still in New York. The Court noted that Tejas' declaration indicates that the Decedent was very technologically savvy and that he beleives she would have signed the document electronically. The Court also stated that the declaration is in support of the petition. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Trust and Determine Ownership of Trust Assets (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Confirm Trust And Determine Ownership Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Continued To Sep-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Trust And Determine Ownership Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Continued To Sep-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kulwinder Bains by CourtCall for Nakul Narayan.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the petition and evidence, that there was enough presented here with regard to the personal property, but that the Court had a couple of issues with regard to the real property. The Court addressed Probate Code 15206, stated that it needed more information, and that it understood that Mr Narayan found the documents on the computer. Mr. Bains stated that Mr. Narayan found the documents on his Mother's personal computer at her residence. The Court stated that it wanted more information on how Mr. Narayan obtained the documents on the computer, that it also wanted to know if the decendent kept hard copies of documents, that it wants to know the efforts that Mr. Narayan went through to find any written documents, that it wants to know what research was done such as looking for a safety deposit box, and that it also wanted to know what kind of electronic signature was used & where the signature was maintained. Mr. Bains stated that he could get a declaration on file. The Court stated that this would provide the Court comfort that this document is trustworthy and authentic, that it wants the history of the document, and that it wanted a quick points and authorities as to why this document should be accepted. Mr. Bains stated that he was the estate planning attorney, that these are the same documents that he emailed to Ms, Narayan, and given Ms. Narayan's grave condition at the time that she just couldn't get a notary and two witnesses. Based on the statements by Mr. Bains the Court requested that Mr. Bains submit a declaration including the information presented in open court today. The declaration is to include the fact that Mr. Bains sent Ms. Narayan the documents, that this is the exact language that was sent, and that these are the exact documents that were sent to Ms. Narayan. The Court also requested consents from the decendent's Sons. The Court stated that the evidence is strong especially with counsel's offer of proof today but it does want to make sure that the electronic signature is adequate. The Court reiterated that it wants a declaration, the points and authorties, and the consents filed. The Court then continued the Petition to Confirm Trust and Determine Ownership of Trust Assets to September 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the Court will have the submissions by that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
